Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Airbase raises $5 million to tackle spectrum bottleneck as FCC eyes new space uses

New York-based software startup Airbase emerged from stealth March 25 after raising $5 million to modernize how governments coordinate radio frequencies used by satellites, 5G networks and other wireless systems. The post Airbase raises $5 million to tackle spectrum bottleneck as FCC eyes new spa...

Airbase's spectrum coordination software addresses a critical infrastructure gap as FCC allocates frequencies to competing space-based services, potentially accelerating deployment of satellite constellations and terrestrial networks.

See SpaceNews →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

JetBlue Flight Halted After Bizarre Runway Encounter With Coyote

The aircraft did not suffer any damage and was back in the air shortly after the incident, transporting passengers to their original destination.

Wildlife incursions at major airports represent an underappreciated operational disruption risk that airlines must factor into scheduling and contingency planning.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

Warehouse drones are quietly transforming alcohol distribution across America

In a move that shows just how fast warehouse automation is evolving, Corvus Robotics has teamed up with Southern Glazer’s Wine & Spirits to scale autonomous inventory drones across one of the largest beverage distribution networks in the US. more…

Autonomous warehouse drones are enabling rapid scaling of inventory operations within highly regulated distribution networks, signaling accelerated adoption of aerial robotics beyond traditional delivery use cases.

See DroneDJ →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Musk wants to go to the moon. But how will he build his ‘self-growing city’?

It goes without saying that we shouldn’t take everything Elon Musk says too seriously. There are whole websites dedicated to those times when he has ‘talked the talk’ but failed to ‘walk the walk’ — as well as those times when he has been factually untrue. But his latest claim, which is that he w...

Musk's lunar city concept requires solving unprecedented challenges in in-situ resource utilization and autonomous construction that extend far beyond current spacecraft engineering capabilities.

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must document their risk acceptance rationale for residual risks after implementing controls, creating auditable evidence of deliberate safety decisions.

Drones Mar 25, 2026

Bay Area next as Wing expands drone delivery network

The Wing team has announced plans to expand its ultra-fast residential drone delivery service across the San Francisco Bay Area in the coming months, marking a full-circle moment for the company that was originally born inside Google X back in 2012. more…

Wing's Bay Area expansion demonstrates drone delivery scaling beyond initial markets, validating commercial viability of autonomous delivery infrastructure in dense urban regions.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

14+ Hours Nonstop: Delta Air Lines Reveals Brand New Long-Haul Route

Delta Air Lines has disclosed another new long-haul route: Los Angeles to Manila. While it has not been officially announced yet, it was revealed in the airline's response to Philippine Airlines' and its application to the US Department of Transportation to begin flights from Manila to Chicago O'...

Delta's Manila route signals intensifying transpacific competition as carriers vie for expanding Asia-US market share through strategic DOT applications.

See Simple Flying →

Air Cargo Mar 25, 2026

Meet The New King Of Cargo: Atlas Air Orders 20 Airbus A350Fs

Well, it looks like in a few years’ time, the United States will have ANOTHER A350-operator alongside Delta… but it won’t be flying passengers! That’s because Airbus announced on March 16th that Atlas Air Worldwide has placed a firm order for 20 Airbus A350F freighters, making it the largest cust...

Atlas Air's 20 A350F order signals a major shift toward widebody freighters for ultra-long-range cargo routes, positioning Airbus to compete directly with Boeing's 777F dominance.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

SMS Declaration of Compliance is now mandatory for operators, requiring documented safety management systems that the FAA will actively audit for compliance verification.

Porter's expansion into Montreal Metropolitan Airport signals regional carrier consolidation around secondary hubs, potentially reshaping competitive dynamics in Eastern Canada's short-haul market.

TSA staffing losses during shutdown threaten airport security screening capacity, potentially creating operational disruptions and delays across commercial aviation networks.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Air France Has Ended Airbus A380 Flights On 18 Routes: Full List

Uncover the hidden gems among the Air France's A380 routes, including the five airports that only saw the carrier's superjumbos.

Air France's A380 network consolidation reveals that five airports lost exclusive superjumbo service, signaling a broader industry shift toward smaller wide-bodies on mid-haul routes.

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter accident

Wire strike fatalities persist despite pilot overconfidence in reaction time, indicating that enhanced detection systems rather than pilot awareness alone are critical for rotorcraft safety.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Military Mar 25, 2026

Photos of A-10s Used to Strike Iranian Naval Vessels Show Video Game-Inspired Nicknames

Images shared by U.S. Central Command suggest that at least some A-10s taking part in Operation Epic Fury carry new video game-inspired nicknames, an operationally irrelevant detail but one likely to interest spotters and modelers. As we already reported a few days ago, the U.S. Air Force A-10 Th...

A-10 Thunderbolt II aircraft involved in recent Middle Eastern operations display video game-inspired nose art, reflecting pilot culture and morale practices that persist despite modern digital warfare capabilities.

Singapore Airlines' daily commitment to Western Sydney Airport signals carrier confidence in the facility's operational readiness and represents a strategic pivot to capture growth in Australia's emerging secondary hub market.

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via taxi

Runway exit design at major hubs like LAX creates systematic incursion risks that require pilot awareness and potentially infrastructure modifications to prevent accidents.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Pave Space raises $40 million to develop European heavy kickstage

Swiss startup Pave Space has raised $40 million to develop an orbital transfer vehicle that could move satellites from LEO to their final destinations in hours instead of months. The post Pave Space raises $40 million to develop European heavy kickstage appeared first on SpaceNews.

European satellite operators could reduce deployment timelines by 90% through in-orbit transfer services, creating new market opportunities for responsive space missions.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

What Are The Benefits Of Flying Emirates Business Class?

The benefits extend beyond the cabin and begin well before boarding.

Premium cabin experience now encompasses pre-flight ground services, expanding competitive differentiation beyond traditional in-flight amenities for business travelers.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations

NASA, concerned about slow development of commercial markets, is considering revamping its strategy for supporting the creation of commercial space stations to succeed the International Space Station. The post NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations appeared first on SpaceNews.

NASA's pivot toward incentivizing commercial space station development signals growing urgency to maintain continuous orbital infrastructure access beyond ISS retirement.

See SpaceNews →

Industry Mar 25, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airline, airport, and flight domains can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impact as air traffic demand continues rising.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Press Release: Immfly, Wizz Air, and gateretail partner on digital cabin

Immfly, Wizz Air, and gateretail have launched an integrated digital cabin ecosystem that unifies onboard retail, passenger and crew connectivity, and operational data into a single platform, marking a significant step forward in the digital transformation of Europe’s commercial aviation sector. ...

Integrated digital cabin platforms consolidate retail, connectivity, and operations into single systems, reducing complexity while creating new revenue opportunities for low-cost carriers.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

United Airlines eases long-haul travel for families with new economy beds

Long-haul flights for families may become a little easier in the future after United Airlines unveiled plans to… The post United Airlines eases long-haul travel for families with new economy beds appeared first on AeroTime.

United's economy bed initiative targets the underserved family segment, potentially creating competitive pressure for rivals to enhance economy comfort offerings on long-haul routes.

Military Mar 25, 2026

DOT&E Says No Combat-Capable TR-3-Configured F-35s Were Delivered in FY2025

The latest Director, Operational Test & Evaluation report says no combat-capable F-35 TR-3 aircraft have been delivered to the U.S. military until September 2025 because of the truncated software. The recently released 2025 Director, Operational Test & Evaluation (DOT&E) annual report brought ano...

F-35 TR-3 delivery delays stem from incomplete software maturation, signaling continued integration challenges affecting fighter modernization timelines.

See The Aviationist →

Wizz Air's partnership with Immfly enables low-cost carriers to monetize connectivity and retail services previously unavailable to budget operators.

United's Relax Row strategy signals that airlines are monetizing comfort increments within economy rather than forcing upgrades, potentially fragmenting the traditional cabin hierarchy and creating new revenue tiers between standard and premium economy.

Kona Storms Flood Oʻahu

Back-to-back subtropical cyclones in March fueled destructive flash flooding on several of the Hawaiian Islands.

Extreme weather events like back-to-back subtropical cyclones pose operational challenges for Hawaiian airports, requiring enhanced weather monitoring and potential flight disruption contingencies.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

How Much Does It Cost To Fly A Boeing 747-400 For 1 Hour?

The Boeing 747-400 is both more expensive and less expensive to operate than modern aircraft with fuel spikes adding to the cost range.

Volatile fuel prices create unpredictable operating costs for legacy widebodies, making economic viability highly dependent on market conditions rather than aircraft age alone.

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Digitizing borders could free up space to reimagine #PaxEx

Border control and lost baggage are the two remaining “pain points” when it comes to creating seamless air passenger journeys. But smart technology deployment and closer cooperation between airlines, airports and regulators could help resolve these issues and free up enough space to transform the...

Eliminating physical border infrastructure through digitization could reclaim valuable airport terminal space for premium passenger amenities and commercial opportunities, addressing a structural constraint on the modern airport experience.

U.S. Considering Deployment of 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East

Senior officials are reportedly considering the deployment of the Immediate Response Force, which includes a Battalion Combat Team of the 82nd Airborne Division, according to U.S. media. After increasing rumors regarding a deployment of U.S. Army troops being in progress, new reports now mention ...

Military airlift demand may surge if the 82nd Airborne deploys, creating significant transport requirements for strategic cargo and personnel movement.
